summary: Tested CalcAuto AiPro Corrections Memory System. All 9 backend tests passed. The corrections system correctly: 1) Lists corrections via GET /api/corrections with total count and structure, 2) Saves corrections to program_corrections collection when Excel import has modified values, 3) Deletes all corrections via DELETE /api/corrections/all?password=Liana2018, 4) Each correction contains brand, model, trim, year, corrected_values (consumer_cash, alternative_consumer_cash, bonus_cash, option1_rates, option2_rates), and corrected_at timestamp.
critical_code_review_comments: import_wizard.py correctly implements GET /api/corrections endpoint that returns {total, corrections} with all required fields
programs.py import_programs_excel() correctly saves corrections to program_corrections collection via upsert when values change
import_wizard.py delete_all_corrections() correctly validates ADMIN_PASSWORD before deleting
Correction structure properly stores consumer_cash, alternative_consumer_cash, bonus_cash, option1_rates, option2_rates in corrected_values
find_best_correction() uses normalize_correction_model() and normalize_correction_str() for flexible matching
seed_data_creation: Test data created and restored during tests (no persistent changes)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Corrections Memory System fully tested. Key endpoints: GET /api/corrections returns {total: int, corrections: []}. POST /api/programs/import-excel saves corrections to program_corrections when values are modified. DELETE /api/corrections/all?password=Liana2018 clears all corrections. Each correction has: brand, model, trim, year, corrected_values: {consumer_cash, alternative_consumer_cash, bonus_cash, option1_rates, option2_rates}, corrected_at. The system uses flexible matching via normalize_correction_model() and normalize_correction_str() when applying corrections during PDF import (POST /api/save-programs).
